node.title: Lost Despite 500+ LeetCode - Need FAANG Prep Roadmap (Working Professional)

article.title: Lost Despite 500+ LeetCode - Need FAANG Prep Roadmap (Working Professional)
article.content:
**Background:**
- Currently working (5 Days - 9Hours) (In Office)
- 500+ LeetCode problems solved
- Contest: 2/4 problems typically
- Failed: Uber, Urban Company, Amazon interviews
- Problem: Panic on new/unseen problems

## Questions

### 1. How to prepare for Google from scratch?
Need focused roadmap - topics, order, depth?

### 2. Is Competitive Programming required?
Should I grind CP ratings or stick to LeetCode? Since grinding CP is difficult to manage with job.

### 3. How to handle interview panic?
I freeze when I see unfamiliar problems. How to stay calm?

### 4. How to revise effectively?
With 500+ problems, I forget approaches. What's the strategy?

## What I'm Willing to Do
- 100% commitment for 3-6 months
- Start from scratch if needed
- Mock interviews, system design, whatever it takes

**Please share your journey/strategies that worked for you. Especially need help with staying calm and effective revision.**

post.content:
I've failed Amazon 7 times (twice in 2025), Google, Meta, Microsoft and many others, but, I landed a job at an AI startup.
Keeping it short:

1. How to prepare for Google from scratch? Kickoff topic wise. Identify your weak areas. Grind more there.
2. Is Competitive Programming required? Kind-of. That time pressure's gonna help you come up with algos that are faster.
3. How to handle interview panic? Interviewers are your friends. When they ask something, assume they are completely new. Help them by answering what you think, maybe like a young-professor in a friendly way. If you do not know, accept and report them you have shallow knowledge.
4. How to revise effectively? Solve POTD and Online Assessments Daily. Most of what you've solved are repeated. So trying them again would be a revision. Additionally see if you can come up with a better solution.

LeetCoding won't guarantee you anything, but it helps you build muscle memory. Keep practicing everyday, when you see a question, you already know the best approach.

Pro Tip: 
1. If it takes >20 mins (Medium/Hard), look up for solution and understand logic. Then try to type the logic by yourself.
2. Don't loose hope. Remember if things aren't working when you're doing everything you can, is delayed but not denied.
3. Genuineness, accepting failure and urge to learn from it will definitely take you there.

In the same boat and I hope that helps. All the very best!!

Hi ,
I currently work at Meta , and have cracked google , salesforce , amazon gs , atlassian and many other company in past 
so let me tell you my thought process of what i do

As you have already solved 500 question I would not give you generic advice like do this dsa sheet do that sheet , do these topics i think you already know that , so lets say what is the best value for time , let me tell you how I prepare taking google as an example then  answer your question

How I prepared for google:

Usually for companies that ask a lot of past questions I simply take Leetcode premium and solve those question in that way I knpw exactly what topics they ask what optimizations are they looking at etc.

Google was different as they dont generally ask Leetcode copy paste questions so this is what i did

1) Starting 1 week just made strategy (gathering info from leetcode discussion dumping it all in a doc ) - I usually do this for all my interviews -
![Screenshot 2026-02-17 at 18.08.11.png](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/51727b7d-8a7e-4647-bc24-23e30fec885f_1771331955.2851505.png)


After this step you usually know important topics for a company - for google it is hands down dp , binary search and graph
I also get to know if i need to do advance topics or not 
example when I prepared for Meta , i knew they dont ask dynamic programming but focus lot on topics like recurssion and backtracking
for google they do ask topics like segment trees , tries etc so i decided not to skip it

2) Now start solving question (next 2-3wks) - mix of past tagged + the one i found in latest discussions and again as I am big believer of making notes I keep track

![Screenshot 2026-02-17 at 18.04.03.png](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/3d6118c1-1c67-477d-992a-e254cd6ca15a_1771331664.5234447.png)


3) Know you know how to revise bcz you already have a list of questions that you had difficulty in the first place 

![Screenshot 2026-02-17 at 17.56.25.png](https://assets.leetcode.com/users/images/bf89099d-365e-408b-8235-d089107bb21b_1771331197.5325942.png)

4) Just Grind , but strategically using the knowlege gained time yourself (there is no shortcut but now you know the best ROI of your time)

**** ANSWERING YOUR QUESTIONS NOW ******


1. How to prepare for Google from scratch?
I think already explained about that but leaving a link to my prep doc (just make a copy I might make it non public soon)
https://docs.google.com/document/d/1o7GzRgR76pS9HPsbjU9sCjp0UBDIgIVkLs8k22pzsc4/edit?tab=t.0#heading=h.z27jrtkeqj5i

2) 2. Is Competitive Programming required? - Short ans No , long ans No (do only if you enjoy it)

3. How to handle interview panic? - I still panic after cracking and interviewing so much (your best bet is to be overprepared and accept some things are out of control)

4. How to revise effectively?
I think already answers - JUST MAKE NOTES - you know yourself best , you know your wk topics , questions that took more time , questions that you needed assistance in first place (you can answer this best for yourself , what what helps is data points)

5) What I'm Willing to Do
I am rooting for you , my leetcode profile has a linkedin attached msg me if you need more help , mock system design interview etc 

HAPPY to help!!!

hope this was useful
you need mock interviews and to train yourself more, forego your rating and ego and just accept it for what it is
Can you reach out to me on discord, i am at a similar stage and looking for partners for mock interviews, username: independent_lab
